title: Keep Memories Accurate: - Write Details Down: Capture Events as Soon as Possible to
subtitle: Balance Leveling and Sharpening
description:
To keep memories accurate:
- Write details down: Capture events as soon as possible to avoid losing information.
- Check for gaps: Ask yourself what might be missing in your memory.
- Avoid exaggeration: Be mindful of which details you emphasize when retelling stories.

Example: After a meeting, jot down key points immediately before memories get distorted.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Start by tracking your current memory capture habits. Note how many interactions you capture daily and your confidence level in those captures. This will help you measure improvement over time.

howTo.title: Implement the three-field method
howTo.summary: After each interaction, capture the details using the three fields: Who, What, and When. This structured approach helps preserve accuracy and reduces memory distortion.

howTo.title: Schedule verification for low-confidence items
howTo.summary: If you rate your confidence in a capture as 3 or lower, schedule a follow-up within 24-72 hours to verify the details. This prevents inaccuracies from solidifying into memory.

howTo.title: Run the 3-day experiment
howTo.summary: Commit to using the capture method consistently for three days. Track your captures and confidence levels to observe any changes in your memory accuracy.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and refine your memory capture process. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/cognitive-bias-memory-capture/.
